---
title: "Adaptive Trial Design for the RAS-Inhibitor Era"
subtitle: "Combinations, Resistance, and Biomarker-Guided Therapy in GI Oncology"
---

::: {.callout-important}
## Slides and handout

[View the slides](ras-era-adaptive-trials.html){.btn .btn-primary} &nbsp;
[Download the PDF](../assets/downloads/ras-era-adaptive-trials.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

Talk given at the NCI GI SPORE Meeting 2026 by Naim Rashid, PhD (Department of Biostatistics, Lineberger Comprehensive Cancer Center, UNC Chapel Hill).
:::

## Thesis

New RAS therapies need new kinds of trials. Methods to optimize combination doses are established. Designs that track resistance and adapt the trial in response are enrolling. What's missing is making those designs practical to calibrate and run.

## Argument in three movements

1. **The clinical case.** Daraxonrasib's RASolute 302 result reshapes the GI oncology trial landscape, but monotherapy has visible ceilings — and the combinations now in flight need design choices that 3+3 cannot make.

2. **The dose-finding problem.** Project Optimus has shifted regulatory expectations from MTD to optimized biological dose (OBD). The Bayesian model-assisted toolkit — BOIN, BOIN-COMB, TITE-BOIN, BOIN12, POCRM, BLRM, U-BOIN — is the operating system. PANGEA shows what U-BOIN looks like in a basal-like PDAC erlotinib + gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel trial.

3. **Resistance is the next problem.** Each patient is a moving target. Genomic resistance is catchable by ctDNA; non-genomic plasticity needs serial biopsies and transcriptomics. ADAPT/EVOLVE is the federally funded breast-cancer trial template that generalizes to RAS-backbone PDAC combinations.

The closing tool: **BATON** (Bayesian Adaptive Trial OptimizatioN). Constrained Bayesian optimization for calibrating modern adaptive designs. Days instead of months — so design choice becomes about what's right for the trial, not what's feasible in the timeline.

## Companion materials

- [BATON R package](https://github.com/naimurashid/BATON) — open-source calibration tool
- BATON manuscript (Young et al., under review at JASA Applications and Case Studies)

## Acknowledgments

EVOLVE is led by Lisa Carey at UNC, with multiple-PIs Charles Perou (UNC), Ian Krop and Eric Winer (Yale), Antonio Wolff (Johns Hopkins), and Naim Rashid (UNC). PANGEA is led by Ashwin Somasundaram (UNC). Methods collaborators include Stephen Bates (MIT), Ying Yuan (MD Anderson), and Qian Cao (FDA CDRH). The EVOLVE Statistical Working Group includes Susan Hilsenbeck (Baylor), Nabihah Tayob (DFCI), and Ruizhe Chen (Hopkins). The Rashid Lab work on BATON is led by Amber Young.

Funding: NCI P50-CA257911 (UNC SToP SPORE), ARPA-H 140D042590009, DOD HT9425241103100121, NCI U01-CA274298.
